title: Add registry mirror support
body:
Vendor in the latest containers/image to support registry mirrors.
github.com/etcd-io/bbolt has to be vendored as direct dependency.

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
@saschagrunert Now that the PR is merged, what do you want to do with this PR?
@rhatdan The changes introduced with this PR are still needed when vendoring the latest contianers/image commit. On the other side we could also wait for the next released version.
Ok, I want to get a released version of containers/image out soon, so we can vendor it into buildah and release a new version of buildah.

> Ok, I want to get a released version of containers/image out soon, so we can vendor it into buildah and release a new version of buildah.

Alright, so I guess we can leave this open and just adapt the vendored version after a release. Please note that this new version of containers/image contains not backwards compatible changes, like the renaming of `URL` to `Location` in sysregistriesv2. So I guess it is time for 2.0.0 there. :innocent:

Let's wait with releasing a new c/image until the remaining issues are ironed out. I don't think we do us a favor promoting the mirror support in a rush and forcing users to change existing deployments with hypothetical follow up releases.
Yes I don't think we will vendor in an update for container/image before the next buildah release.  That way we can keep it simple.  If we decide not to rebase what do we loose?

* docs: containers-transports(5) manpage
* Add registry mirror support to docker client
* Switch to github.com/etcd-io/bbolt
* storage: fix UID detection in a namespace
* blobinfocache: correctly detect the UID in a namespace
* bump version to 1.7.0-dev
* release v1.6.0
* pkg/docker/config: Remove GetUserLoggedIn

This should not affect Buildah (famous last words?)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 7ae362b) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
